mission.title: Install and Configure Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
mission.description: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of Kubernetes clusters on AWS. It is ideal for users who want to leverage AWS's infrastructure without managing the underlying servers. Key trade-offs include reliance on AWS services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the AWS CLI
steps.description:
The AWS CLI is required to interact with AWS services, including EKS. Ensure you have the latest version installed.

```bash

pip install awscli --upgrade --user

```

> Install or upgrade AWS CLI to the latest version.

steps.title: Create an EKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates an EKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count in a chosen region.

```bash

aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321

```

> Replace YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID with your AWS account ID and subnet IDs with your actual subnet IDs.

steps.title: Update kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command updates your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to connect to your new EKS cluster.

```bash

aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster

```

> This sets up your local kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your EKS cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Set Up Monitoring and Autoscaling
steps.description:
Configure Cluster Autoscaler and monitoring tools for better resource management.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/aws/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes/kube-state-metrics/releases/latest/download/kube-state-metrics-1.9.7.yaml

```

> This installs the Cluster Autoscaler.
> This installs kube-state-metrics for monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the EKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the EKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

aws eks delete-cluster --name my-cluster

```

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
After deleting the cluster, check for and manually delete any orphaned resources such as load balancers and EBS volumes.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?contains(LoadBalancerName, `my-cluster`)]'

```

> List load balancers associated with the cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the EKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your cluster state and configurations. Then, perform the upgrade and verify the status.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml

aws eks update-cluster-version --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that nodes are in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: No resources found in default namespace
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the resources have not been created or are in a different namespace.

**Fix:**
Check the correct namespace with 'kubectl get all --all-namespaces'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Cluster is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the cluster is not running or the kubeconfig is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the cluster is running and your kubeconfig is correctly set up.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Insufficient permissions to perform the operation
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the IAM role associated with your user does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Update the IAM role to include the required permissions for EKS operations.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved integration with AWS services.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs in favor of newer versions.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure to review the deprecation notices and update your configurations accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
